citations.description: Only mentions 'PRESENT-ONLY' and 'byte-identical record' — does not document the rollout posture for pre-upgrade clones.
test_hmac_record_is_additively_unchanged: explicitly does NOT assert HMAC certification due to public_state stripping the hex. Confirms additive schema property but not 'still HMAC-verifies'.
Module-level docstring: no mention of opcert rollout posture or pre-upgrade-clone behavior.
Module-level docstring: no rollout note for pre-upgrade clones.

findings.criterion: The record-schema extension is additive (present-only): a test asserts a legacy HMAC `sign_manifest` record is byte-unchanged (no `envelope`/bound keys) and still HMAC-verifies, so existing HMAC attestations are untouched. Rollout note documented: op-cert events are preserved-but-unverifiable on pre-upgrade clones (read as unsigned) — upgrade verify/reconcile hosts first (as with SSHSIG authorship / `TAG_DELTA`).
findings.detail: Two sub-parts of criterion 4 are not fully satisfied. (1) 'Rollout note documented': no code comment, module-level docstring, or docs file in scope contains the required note that op-cert SIGNATURE events are preserved-but-unverifiable on pre-upgrade clones (read as UNSIGNED) and that verify/reconcile hosts must be upgraded first. The comment in `_processors.py` at line 600-601 covers only the additive schema property ('PRESENT-ONLY'), not the rollout posture for pre-upgrade clone behavior. No docs file (signing.py module docstring, opcert.py module docstring, or any ADR/CHANGELOG) contains this note. (2) 'still HMAC-verifies': `test_hmac_record_is_additively_unchanged` (heldout file lines 105-117) explicitly skips asserting HMAC certification because `public_state` strips the hex signature before the test can read it — the test comment acknowledges this gap. The test does confirm the record shape is structurally a valid HMAC record (algorithm=HMAC-SHA256, no opcert fields), and existing HMAC tests in other files cover HMAC verification generally, but no test in this ticket's scope exercises the full HMAC certify path after the opcert extension was introduced.
findings.remediation: Add a code comment or module-level docstring note (e.g. in signing.py's `sign_opcert_manifest` or opcert.py) explicitly stating: 'Op-cert SIGNATURE events are preserved-but-unverifiable on pre-upgrade clones (they reduce to a record with no HMAC signature and are read as UNSIGNED — the old reducer has no asymmetric verifier). Rollout: upgrade verify/reconcile hosts first, as with SSHSIG authorship and TAG_DELTA.' Also add a test (or extend the heldout test) that calls `signing.verify_record` on an HMAC record produced AFTER the opcert extension and asserts it still certifies (verdict='certified') with the correct HMAC key.
findings.title: Rollout note not documented in code; HMAC-verifies not directly asserted in test

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
